assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects terminationGracePeriodSeconds during pod eviction, but surge upgrades have a max eviction timeout
assertions.reason: Response explains that GKE's default surge upgrade timeout (1 hour) will forcibly terminate jobs and mentions setting terminationGracePeriodSeconds ≥ 600 seconds in the job configuration example

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE's default pod eviction timeout during surge upgrade is 1 hour — far less than 8-16 hour jobs
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'GKE's default surge upgrade timeout (1 hour) << job duration' and 'default 1-hour pod eviction timeout will forcibly terminate them'

assertions.expectation: Recommends pausing new batch job submissions before starting the upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes steps to 'Prevent new jobs from scheduling on old nodes' using kubectl cordon and mentions 'Verify no new jobs scheduled during maintenance' in the pre-upgrade coordination section

assertions.expectation: Advises waiting for in-flight jobs to complete naturally before upgrading their node pool
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Wait for current jobs to complete naturally' and 'When all jobs complete, upgrade the empty pool' in the cordon-and-wait strategy

assertions.expectation: Suggests using node affinity or taints to isolate batch workloads on a dedicated node pool
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed commands for creating a dedicated batch node pool with node-labels and node-taints, plus corresponding nodeSelector and tolerations configuration for jobs

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading non-batch node pools first while batch jobs complete on the old pool
assertions.reason: Response includes a phased approach: Phase 1 (Control plane), Phase 2 (Non-batch node pools), and Phase 3 (Batch pool upgrade after jobs complete)

assertions.expectation: Mentions scheduling the upgrade during a natural gap in batch processing if one exists
assertions.reason: Response mentions coordinating 'Only when no active jobs (coordinate with job scheduler)' and includes timeline management with 'Batch pool: Only when no active jobs'

assertions.expectation: Provides commands for cordoning batch nodes to prevent new scheduling while jobs finish
assertions.reason: Response provides specific kubectl cordon commands: 'kubectl cordon -l workload=batch' and later 'kubectl uncordon -l workload=batch' when upgrade completes
